fields.description:
# Project-supplied review criteria + project-invariant compliance (unified cross-gate registry)

## Context

rebar's review gates (plan-review; code-review, epic `b744`, in progress) ship a fixed,
built-in set of criteria. Client projects have their own standards and invariants — naming,
layering, banned APIs, architectural rules — the built-in set can't express. This epic lets a
**project supply its own review criteria** and declare **architectural invariants** the gates
check, **beyond** the built-in set.

This is a **cross-cutting** capability: it must serve **plan-review, code-review, and any
future criteria-based gate** through **one shared registry**, not a plan-review-local feature.
(Spun out of `5fd2` as follow-on F8; the config-UX sibling is `55de`/F5.)

**Posture (inherited from `5fd2`, binding):** coach-not-block, advisory-by-default, fail-open
(ironclad), polyglot / broad diverse client base, convergent-by-construction, and **reuse
existing machinery — do NOT invent a plugin system or a new DSL**. The gate's own A1/G6
criteria check for NIH / over-engineering, so this design is deliberately reuse-first.

## Design decisions (settled in brainstorm; see rationale comment)

1. **Unified criterion data model + shared registration/discovery/schema/eval plumbing;
   per-gate application.** Gate-scoped routing lives *inside* the shared record (plan-review
   `applies_at`, code-review `applies_to`); the two gates' postures do NOT converge.
2. **Two authoring forms, no project code in the gate:** DET "invariants" = declarative
   pattern rules (ast-grep/OpenGrep) via the existing grounding scan facade
   (`.rebar/detectors/`); LLM criteria = prompt files via the prompt machinery
   (`.rebar/prompts/`).
3. **Any criterion (DET or LLM) may block** — `--force`-escapable, folds into the signed
   (kind-keyed) attestation coverage, can never rebind a built-in.
4. **Blocking is project-owned policy.** rebar surfaces the same eval/calibration instruments
   it uses on its built-ins (fixtures + discrimination + a calibration view); the maintainer
   decides blocking + threshold. Trust is the maintainer's. ("Eval-gated the same way the
   built-ins are" = access to the same tooling, not a mandatory rebar-imposed pass.)
5. **Reuse existing formats** — prompt front-matter, the routing-index format, Inspect-AI
   fixtures, the scan facade. No new formats.
6. **Explicit activation in config; presence != active.** Routing/threshold in a `.rebar/`
   overlay (merged over built-ins like `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`); namespaced + additive;
   a project id can't rebind a built-in; the overlay may re-tune or **disable** a built-in (a
   disable **reduces certified coverage and is recorded** in the attestation).
7. **Eval tooling in scope** = fixture/discrimination harness + calibration/agreement view.
   **Out** = the REVIEW_RESULT FP-replay (F1, deferred; gate-agnostic — project criteria
   inherit it later).
8. **An "invariant" is just a blocking criterion** (usually a DET pattern-rule). One
   mechanism; the two original ACs collapse. UI may label blocking DET pattern-rules
   "invariants."
9. **Visual editor extended** to author project criteria (reuses `/library/create` +
   `create/update_prompt`).

## What exists vs. what we build (grounded to file:line, 2026-06-30)

**Already built (reuse):** project pattern-rule discovery + execution (`.rebar/detectors/`
union, last-wins, fail-open, three-valued evidence); project eval-fixture format + discovery
(`.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml`, project-wins); discrimination scorers + Cohen's kappa +
parity/agreement metrics; editor criterion-authoring CRUD (`create_prompt(kind=criterion)`,
`/library/create`, `/library` picker); `.rebar/prompts/` rubric override; the
`.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` additive-merge precedent; the shared three-pass kernel
(`review_kernel/`).

**Net-new (build):**
- Gate actually *runs* an authored criterion — today it is listable-but-inert (`CANONICAL_LLM`
  frozenset + packaged-only `criteria_routing.json`). Needs `effective_criteria(repo_root)`
  through the loader + coverage guard + `workflow_ops` vocabulary, and an **uncached,
  repo_root-keyed** `.rebar/` routing overlay (the loaders are `@lru_cache`d with no repo key —
  a naive merge leaks one repo's routing to all).
- A gate consumer for Engine-B `scan` (nothing calls it today; the DET floor uses only
  `refute_absence`) — an evidence->finding adapter + wiring.
- A per-criterion eval runner (eval is per-prompt whole-suite; the live solver is a closed
  hardcoded dispatch; the CLI is validate-only) + the calibration view (metrics exist;
  rendering is net-new).
- Claim-gate attestation invalidation on criteria change (`claim_gate_check` never re-compares
  the routing version — must add a regver/effective-criteria check).
- Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview (no prompt-execution endpoint exists today).
- A routing derivation/validation pipeline (`criteria_routing.json` is hand-maintained; no
  generator/drift gate today).

## Dependency

**Blocked on `b744` (code-review capability).** b744 is adding a parallel
`code_review/registry.py` + `criteria_routing.json`; this epic unifies plan-review's and
code-review's registries into the shared `rebar.llm.criteria` layer after b744 lands (accepting
temporary duplication rather than interleaving with churning work).

## Success Criteria

- A project can add its own review criteria (DET pattern-rules and LLM prompts) that run in
  plan-review, code-review, and future gates via **one shared registry**.
- A project can declare architectural invariants (blocking DET criteria) the gates check
  compliance against.
- A project **owns the blocking/threshold policy** per criterion, informed by the same
  eval/calibration instruments rebar uses on its built-ins.
- **No project-authored code executes inside the gate**; unsupported stacks fail open.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A shared `rebar.llm.criteria` registry that both plan-review and code-review consume,
      replacing the two parallel registries (depends on `b744`).
- [ ] Project criteria are discovered and **explicitly activated** via config, with
      routing/threshold in a `.rebar/` overlay (namespaced, additive; presence != active).
- [ ] The overlay may re-tune or disable a built-in criterion; a disable is recorded in the
      attestation coverage.
- [ ] Project DET pattern-rule "invariants" run through the existing scan facade and surface as
      (blocking-capable) findings; fail-open when the engine binary is absent.
- [ ] Project LLM prompt criteria run through the shared registry with gate-scoped routing.
- [ ] The eval/discrimination harness + a calibration/agreement view are surfaced per-criterion
      (CLI); FP-replay (F1) is explicitly out of scope.
- [ ] A project criterion may block (project-configured threshold/posture), `--force`-escapable;
      its verdict + coverage fold into the signed attestation; it can never rebind a built-in.
- [ ] Activating/editing/disabling a project criterion invalidates a prior plan-review
      attestation at claim time.
- [ ] The visual editor can author a project criterion (prompt + routing form) and run a live
      must-fire/must-not-fire preview.
- [ ] Docs: how a project authors/activates/evals/blocks on a criterion; the
      invariant = blocking-criterion model.

## Out of scope

- Arbitrary project-authored code in the gate (declarative pattern-rules only).
- Graph-level invariants (transitive layering / cycles / containment) — wrap an existing tool
  later; not a new DSL.
- Baseline/grandfathering — gates are change-scoped (plan/diff), not whole-repo scans.
- REVIEW_RESULT FP-replay calibration (F1); the richer config-UX (`55de`/F5).
fields.title: Project-supplied review criteria + project-invariant compliance (unified cross-gate registry)
